title: Google broke reCAPTCHA for de-googled Android users
summary: The provided text, despite the title "Google broke reCAPTCHA for de-googled Android users," discusses Brave browser. It introduces a new "stripped down option" from Brave, describing it as a "surprisingly coherent product." The article indicates that the relevance and necessity of this Brave option are entirely dependent on the specific type of privacy user.
chinese_title: 谷歌破坏了去谷歌化安卓用户的reCAPTCHA
chinese_summary: 尽管标题为“谷歌为‘去谷歌化’的安卓用户破坏了reCAPTCHA”，但该文本讨论的是Brave浏览器。它介绍了Brave推出的一款新的“精简选项”，并称其为“一个出奇连贯的产品”。文章指出，这个Brave选项的相关性和必要性完全取决于特定类型的隐私用户。

title: Cloudflare to cut about 20% of its workforce
summary:
Cloudflare plans to reduce its workforce by approximately 7% in the coming weeks, which translates to around 500 employees. This move, announced by CEO Matthew Prince in an email to staff and shared in a blog post, aims to make the company "leaner and faster."

The job cuts are not attributed to performance issues, but rather to an overestimation of the company's growth trajectory during a period of rapid hiring. Cloudflare had rapidly expanded its team from 1,000 employees in 2019 to over 3,000 by 2022. The company, which specializes in web infrastructure and security, aims to improve its focus and efficiency.

Impacted employees will receive a minimum of 16 weeks of pay, their Q1 2024 bonus, and equity vesting through February 2024, along with benefits and job search assistance. Cloudflare joins a growing list of tech companies, including Google, Amazon, and Microsoft, that have undertaken significant layoffs recently, signaling a broader trend of workforce adjustments in the industry.
chinese_title: Cloudflare将裁员约20%员工
chinese_summary:
Cloudflare 计划在未来几周内裁员约 7%，相当于约 500 名员工。首席执行官 Matthew Prince 在给员工的电子邮件中宣布了这一举措，并在博客文章中分享，旨在使公司“更精简、更快速”。

此次裁员并非源于业绩问题，而是由于在快速招聘期间高估了公司的增长轨迹。Cloudflare 的团队规模在 2019 年从 1,000 名员工迅速扩大到 2022 年的 3,000 多名。这家专注于网络基础设施和安全的公司，旨在提高其专注度和效率。

受影响的员工将获得至少 16 周的工资、2024 年第一季度奖金、股权归属至 2024 年 2 月，以及福利和求职援助。Cloudflare 加入了包括谷歌、亚马逊和微软在内的越来越多的科技公司行列，这些公司近期都进行了大规模裁员，这预示着行业内劳动力调整的更广泛趋势。

title: Canvas online again as ShinyHunters threatens to leak schools’ data
summary:
The learning platform Canvas is back online after a massive outage caused by a data breach, with the hacking group ShinyHunters claiming responsibility. Students attempting to access the system saw a ransom message from ShinyHunters, which stated they had breached Instructure (Canvas's owner) again and threatened to leak school data if a settlement wasn't negotiated by May 12, 2026. The message included a list of allegedly compromised schools.

Instructure confirmed an "unauthorized actor" made changes to login pages, prompting them to take Canvas offline "out of an abundance of caution." The breach impacted student names, email addresses, ID numbers, and messages. Instructure identified an exploit related to their "Free-For-Teacher" accounts, which they have temporarily shut down.

While most Canvas services are now available, Canvas Beta and Test systems remain in maintenance, and some users face issues logging into Student ePortfolios. ShinyHunters claims its leak site contains data from 9,000 schools, affecting 275 million students, teachers, and staff. Instructure had previously deployed security patches after an earlier breach, but ShinyHunters claimed these were insufficient. The group has a history of major cyberattacks on companies like Ticketmaster and AT&T.
chinese_title: Canvas 再次上线，正值 ShinyHunters 威胁泄露学校数据
chinese_summary:
学习平台Canvas在因数据泄露导致大规模中断后已恢复上线，黑客组织ShinyHunters声称对此负责。试图访问系统的学生看到了ShinyHunters的勒索信息，其中声称他们再次入侵了Instructure（Canvas的所有者），并威胁说，如果未能在2026年5月12日前协商达成和解，他们将泄露学校数据。该信息中还附带了一份据称已被泄露数据的学校名单。

Instructure证实，一名“未经授权的行为者”更改了登录页面，促使他们“出于高度谨慎”而将Canvas下线。此次泄露事件影响了学生的姓名、电子邮件地址、ID号码和信息。Instructure发现了一个与其“教师免费”账户相关的漏洞，并已暂时关闭了这些账户。

虽然大多数Canvas服务现已可用，但Canvas Beta和测试系统仍处于维护状态，并且部分用户在登录学生电子档案时遇到问题。ShinyHunters声称其泄密网站包含来自9,000所学校的数据，影响了2.75亿学生、教师和教职员工。Instructure此前在一次早期泄露事件后部署了安全补丁，但ShinyHunters声称这些补丁不足以解决问题。该组织曾对Ticketmaster和AT&T等公司发动过重大网络攻击。

title: Maybe you shouldn't install new software for a bit
summary:
This very brief "article" consists of a title, "Maybe you shouldn't install new software for a bit," followed by a single line of content in Chinese: "正在确认你是不是机器人！ 加载中...请稍等，我们需要在继续之前检查您的连接安全性。"

Translated, the content reads: "Confirming if you are a robot! Loading...Please wait, we need to check your connection security before continuing."

The summary is that the piece offers a cautionary title advising against installing new software temporarily. The accompanying content describes a typical online security verification process, specifically a bot check and a connection security assessment. While the article does not elaborate on *why* one should avoid installing new software, it uses a common security hurdle as its sole textual example, implicitly linking general online security checks to the broader context of software installation caution.
chinese_title: 也许你最好暂时不要安装新软件。
chinese_summary:
This very brief "article" consists of a title, "Maybe you shouldn't install new software for a bit," followed by a single line of content in Chinese: "正在确认你是不是机器人！ 加载中...请稍等，我们需要在继续之前检查您的连接安全性。"

Translated, the content reads: "Confirming if you are a robot! Loading...Please wait, we need to check your connection security before continuing."

The summary is that the piece offers a cautionary title advising against installing new software temporarily. The accompanying content describes a typical online security verification process, specifically a bot check and a connection security assessment. While the article does not elaborate on *why* one should avoid installing new software, it uses a common security hurdle as its sole textual example, implicitly linking general online security checks to the broader context of software installation caution.

title: Dirty Frag: Universal Linux LPE
summary:
This report details "Dirty Frag," a critical universal Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability affecting all major Linux distributions, allowing an unprivileged user to gain root privileges. Discovered by Hyunwoo Kim, its impact is compared to the previous "Copy Fail" vulnerability.

The public disclosure of Dirty Frag occurred prematurely due to a broken embargo, meaning no official patches or CVEs are currently available. The vulnerability chains two distinct kernel issues related to network functionalities, with specific kernel commit links provided.

A temporary mitigation involves disabling the `esp4`, `esp6`, and `rxrpc` kernel modules by adding rules to `/etc/modprobe.d/dirtyfrag.conf` and then removing them.

The provided proof-of-concept exploit code demonstrates the LPE. It leverages unprivileged user and network namespaces (`CLONE_NEWUSER | CLONE_NEWNET`) and vulnerabilities in the `xfrm` (IPSec) and `rxrpc` kernel modules. The exploit constructs a small ELF payload designed to execute a root shell, then uses network socket operations (involving `xfrm_usersa_info` manipulation and `splice` calls via UDP encapsulation) to write this payload directly into the `/usr/bin/su` binary's page cache. This overwrite replaces the target's initial instructions, allowing subsequent execution of `/usr/bin/su` to grant immediate root access.
chinese_title: 脏碎片：通用 Linux 本地权限提升
chinese_summary:
本报告详细介绍了“Dirty Frag”，这是一个严重的通用本地权限提升（LPE）漏洞，影响所有主流Linux发行版，允许非特权用户获取root权限。该漏洞由Hyunwoo Kim发现，其影响与此前的“Copy Fail”漏洞相当。

由于禁令被违反，Dirty Frag的公开披露过早发生，这意味着目前没有官方补丁或CVE可用。该漏洞串联了两个独立的与网络功能相关的内核问题，并提供了具体的内核提交链接。

一种临时缓解措施是，通过向`/etc/modprobe.d/dirtyfrag.conf`添加规则来禁用`esp4`、`esp6`和`rxrpc`内核模块，然后将其移除。

所提供的概念验证（PoC）漏洞利用代码演示了LPE。它利用了非特权用户和网络命名空间（`CLONE_NEWUSER | CLONE_NEWNET`），以及`xfrm` (IPSec) 和`rxrpc`内核模块中的漏洞。该漏洞利用构建了一个旨在执行root shell的小型ELF载荷，然后使用网络套接字操作（涉及`xfrm_usersa_info`操作和通过UDP封装进行的`splice`调用）将此载荷直接写入`/usr/bin/su`二进制文件的页缓存中。这种覆写替换了目标的初始指令，从而使得`/usr/bin/su`的后续执行能够立即授予root权限。

title: Google Cloud Fraud Defence is just WEI repackaged
summary:
Google Cloud Fraud Defense, launched in May 2026 as "the next evolution of reCAPTCHA," is presented as a repackaging of the previously rejected "Web Environment Integrity" (WEI) proposal from 2023. WEI, which sought to have device hardware cryptographically attest to browser and device integrity, was withdrawn after strong opposition from Mozilla and the Electronic Frontier Foundation, who labeled it "Chrome's Plan to DRM the Web" and a threat to the open internet and user privacy.

Fraud Defense employs a QR code challenge, requiring users to scan with a "modern Android device with Google Play Services installed, or modern iPhone/iPad." The article emphasizes that "Google Play Services installed" is key, as it leverages the Play Integrity API for device attestation—the same core mechanism as WEI. Unlike WEI, Fraud Defense launched commercially without public review.

Key criticisms include:
1.  **Bypassability:** Bots can easily defeat the QR challenge using inexpensive certified Android devices or automated camera setups.
2.  **Phishing Risk:** It trains users to scan QR codes, making them vulnerable to malicious phishing campaigns.
3.  **Exclusion:** Privacy-focused users running custom Android ROMs (e.g., GrapheneOS, LineageOS without Play Services) or browsers like Firefox for Android (which doesn't integrate Play Integrity) are effectively blocked.
4.  **Tracking:** Each successful challenge signals to Google which certified device accessed which site, creating a persistent identifier and accumulating a comprehensive record of users' browsing activity, raising significant privacy concerns.

The article concludes that Fraud Defense is a device attestation system, not merely a reCAPTCHA update. It bypasses prior public review processes to build an attribution system that was previously rejected, while failing to offer a robust solution against bots.
chinese_title: 谷歌云防欺诈不过是重新包装的WEI。
chinese_summary:
Google Cloud 欺诈防御于2026年5月推出，被宣传为“reCAPTCHA的下一代演进”，实则是对2023年曾被否决的“Web环境完整性”（WEI）提案的重新包装。WEI旨在让设备硬件以加密方式证明浏览器和设备的完整性，但在Mozilla和电子前哨基金会的强烈反对下被撤回，两方均称其为“Chrome的Web数字版权管理（DRM）计划”，并认为它对开放互联网和用户隐私构成威胁。

欺诈防御采用二维码挑战，要求用户使用“安装了Google Play服务的新型安卓设备，或新型iPhone/iPad”进行扫描。文章强调，“安装Google Play服务”是关键，因为它利用Play Integrity API进行设备证明——这与WEI的核心机制相同。与WEI不同的是，欺诈防御在未经公众审查的情况下商业发布。

主要批评包括：
1.  **可规避性：** 机器人可以使用廉价的认证安卓设备或自动化摄像头装置，轻易地规避二维码挑战。
2.  **网络钓鱼风险：** 它培养用户扫描二维码的习惯，使他们容易受到恶意网络钓鱼活动的攻击。
3.  **排他性：** 运行定制安卓ROM（例如GrapheneOS、未安装Play服务的LineageOS）的注重隐私的用户，或使用Firefox for Android（未集成Play Integrity）等浏览器的用户将被有效阻止。
4.  **追踪：** 每一次成功的挑战都会向Google发送信号，告知哪个认证设备访问了哪个网站，从而创建一个持久性标识符，并累积用户的全面浏览活动记录，引发重大的隐私担忧。

文章总结道，欺诈防御是一个设备证明系统，而不仅仅是reCAPTCHA的更新。它规避了此前的公众审查流程，旨在建立一个曾被否决的归因系统，同时未能提供针对机器人的强大解决方案。

title: A web page that shows you everything the browser told it without asking
summary:
The web page "Since You Arrived · Vol. IV" aims to transparently demonstrate the extensive personal data your browser automatically discloses to any website, "without asking." It emphasizes that while this information is constantly disclosed, JavaScript is essential for the page to *show* you what it has learned.

The page utilizes standard browser APIs and documented techniques, not exploits, to gather data. This includes your IP address (used for approximate geolocation via a third-party, though only partially displayed), detailed device specifications (screen, GPU, battery status, language, operating system, installed fonts), and browser characteristics. It highlights font fingerprinting as a strong signal for unique user identification and references research on how battery data can be used for tracking.

A unique "barcode" is generated based on this device data, computed locally in your browser and never transmitted. Crucially, the page clarifies several prevalent tracking methods it *did not* employ, such as canvas fingerprinting, accessing your clipboard, or detecting logged-in accounts on other sites, while noting these are commonly used elsewhere.

The creators stress their commitment to privacy: they do not track, store any data (no cookies, local storage), or log personal information. Only two anonymous events (arrival, finish) are sent to their server, with no identifying data retained. The content is hand-written, not AI-generated. The overarching message is to expose how easily users can be uniquely identified across the web due to inherent browser design, urging awareness of what every page silently learns about you.
